docs.title: Home
docs.text: # Rohit’s AI Architect Playbook  AI architecture + agentic systems — built like a 4x4: **reliable, resilient, and ready for rough terrain**.   This playbook is my daily operating system to learn, test, and document what works in GenAI & Agentic AI.   Audience: Beginners → Experts Style: Practical + Architecture-first Focus: RAG • Agents • Reliability • Security Vibe: AI Lab + Off-road Start with Daily Roadmap Architecture Patterns Decision Records

docs.title: What you’ll find here
docs.text:
Daily

2 GenAI + 2 Agentic concepts per day, explained clearly with examples and exercises.

Weekly

Weekly synthesis: what changed, what mattered, what to adopt, what to ignore.

Patterns

Reusable blueprints: RAG architectures, agent workflows, observability, evals, and guardrails.

Decision Records

“Why we chose this” docs — tradeoffs, risks, mitigations, and what we’d do differently.

docs.title: My operating principle
docs.text: Good AI systems aren’t magic prompts. They’re engineered systems with clear inputs/outputs, tool boundaries, verification, and failure handling.

docs.title: Phase 1 (Weeks 1–4): Architect foundations
docs.text: Models, RAG, agents, memory, evaluation/observability, security, cost.

docs.title: Phase 2 (Weeks 5–8): Enterprise patterns
docs.text: Governance, routing, multi-model strategy, platform design.

docs.title: Phase 3+: Strategy + CTO simulation
docs.text: Signals, org design, portfolio choices, 3-year roadmaps.

docs.title: Daily
docs.text:
Newest entries at the top.

  • 2026-02-25 — 2026-02-25
  • 2026-02-24 — 2026-02-24 No entries yet. The first one will land automatically.

DAILY FRAMEWORK — 2026-02-24

1) Today’s Signals: - 2026-02-23: Meta unveils new AI model that understands sarcasm better than humans. - 2026-02-22: Google launches customizable AI co-pilot for coding on mobile devices. - 2026-02-20: EU passes new regulation on AI explainability in public decision systems. - 2026-02-18: OpenAI releases multi-modal agent that blends vision, text, and code generation. - 2026-02-17: Microsoft integrates AI agents into Teams for real-time project management. - 2026-02-15: NVIDIA announces breakthrough AI chip with 10x energy efficiency. - 2026-02-14: Startup debuts AI-driven design assistant that auto-generates UX flows. - 2026-02-12: Researchers publish new architecture for continuous learning in AI systems.

2) GenAI: - Title: Sarcasm-Detecting Language Models - How do we architect for nuanced language understanding beyond surface semantics? - What data pipelines handle sarcasm examples without overfitting? - How to validate informal or sarcastic language outputs in a production system? - Can transfer learning help scale sarcasm detection across different domains?

  • Title: Multi-Modal Models Going Mainstream
  • What architecture changes support seamless blending of vision, text, and code inputs?
  • How best to optimize latency without sacrificing multi-modal accuracy?
  • How to structure model updates when modalities improve asynchronously?
  • What are best practices for modular but tightly integrated multi-modal architectures?

3) Agentic AI: - Title: Real-Time Project Management Agents - How to design agents that maintain state and context across asynchronous updates? - What protocols ensure secure, reliable interaction between agents and collaboration apps? - How to build fallback mechanisms when the agent misinterprets human instructions? - Can we create metrics to measure agent contribution value in team workflows?

  • Title: Continuous Learning Agents
  • What system architecture supports live model updates with minimal downtime?
  • How do we handle catastrophic forgetting in prolonged continuous learning?
  • How to architect data feedback loops that are trustworthy and unbiased?
  • What monitoring & alerting mechanisms detect performance degradation early?

4) AI Radar: - Title: Quantum AI Accelerators Entering R&D Labs - What are the practical integration points between classical AI models and quantum processors? - How to architect hybrid pipelines balancing quantum speedups with classical stability? - What challenges emerge around data encoding/decoding for quantum systems? - How to future-proof AI infrastructure for incremental quantum hardware adoption?

5) CTO Brief: - AI tech keeps pushing boundaries—today, sarcasm skills and multi-modal fusion lead the charge. - Real-time agents are getting smarter but we need ironclad reliability and trust metrics. - Quantum AI hype is real now — start noodling on hybrid architecture options ASAP.

6) Rohit’s Notes: - What surprises did I notice in today’s signals? - How might these trends shift my current AI architecture strategy? - Any quick wins or experiments to try based on today’s themes? - What was confusing or needs deeper digging? - Connections I didn't see before?

7) Design Drill: Scenario: Build an AI assistant for a legal team that manages contract reviews, flags risky clauses, and suggests rewrites. It should seamlessly handle text, voice notes, and scanned documents.

Constraints: - Must comply with strict data privacy and audit requirements. - Needs to integrate with existing document management systems. - Should provide explainable suggestions for legal decisions. - Real-time collaboration with human lawyers is critical. - Limited budget and timeline (3 months) for MVP delivery.

Guiding Questions: - How should the multi-modal input pipeline be architected for legal texts, voice, and scans? - What AI explainability techniques fit best for risk detection suggestions? - How can we ensure data privacy while enabling deep context-aware analysis? - What incremental rollout strategies reduce risk and gather meaningful user feedback? - How to architect for real-time multi-user collaboration without security trade-offs?

Ready to fill this out and make some moves. Let's see what today brings.
Daily AI Architecture Journal — 2026-02-25

docs.title: 1) Today’s Signals
docs.text:
• 2026-02-24: Apple unveils new AR headset with integrated AI for real-time environment analysis
  • 2026-02-24: OpenAI releases GPT-5 with multimodal memory capabilities
  • 2026-02-23: Tesla launches fleet-wide update for full autonomy in self-driving cars
  • 2026-02-23: Google DeepMind announces agent that can autonomously retrain on new tasks
  • 2026-02-24: EU passes new AI regulation emphasizing transparency and explainability
  • 2026-02-24: Meta debuts AI-powered content moderation agents deployed at scale
  • 2026-02-25: New startup launches AI middleware platform to unify voice, vision, and sensor data
  • 2026-02-25: Researchers publish breakthrough on energy-efficient neuromorphic chips

docs.title: 2) GenAI
docs.text:
A) GPT-5 Multimodal Memory Integration - How to architect a model pipeline that supports persistent, evolving memory across sessions? - What data privacy and security layers are essential for long-term memory storage? - How to optimize latency when fetching multimodal memories (text, image, sensor) in real-time? - What modular components enable seamless updates without retraining full model?

B) AI Middleware Platform for Unified Data - How to design connectors/APIs that reliably handle heterogeneous data (voice, vision, sensor)? - What orchestrator patterns best manage asynchronous multimodal input streams? - What failure modes must be anticipated when fusing different data types in real-time? - How to build an extensible plugin architecture for future sensor types?

docs.title: 3) Agentic AI
docs.text:
A) Autonomous Retraining Agents (DeepMind style) - What triggers should initiate agent’s decision to retrain autonomously? - How to sandbox retraining so it doesn’t destabilize production AI workflows? - What observability metrics effectively monitor autonomous retraining health? - How to enforce ethical guardrails within self-adapting agents?

B) Meta’s AI Moderation Agents - How to architect hierarchical agent layers - local filter vs global review? - How to balance biased-model risk with diversity in training data? - What human-in-the-loop touchpoints improve agent decision accuracy? - How to handle agent transparency and user appeal processes?

docs.title: 4) AI Radar
docs.text: Neuromorphic Chips Boom - Which workloads will gain most from neuromorphic chip adoption? - How to integrate neuromorphic processors as co-processors with existing AI stacks? - What programming paradigms/tools are necessary for efficient neuromorphic dev? - What are early bottlenecks and scaling challenges for production use?

docs.title: 5) CTO Brief
docs.text:
• GPT-5 pushes multimodal memory—think persistent context, not just one-off chats. Ready your data pipelines.
  • Autonomous retraining is here; build robust sandboxes and observability before running loose agents.
  • Neuromorphic hardware promises huge energy savings—start experimenting on niche workloads ASAP.

docs.title: 6) Rohit’s Notes
docs.text:
• What are the biggest unknowns in multimodal memory persistence?
  • How might decentralized data storage change my model architectures?
  • What minimal viable safety nets can prevent rogue retraining agents?
  • How to quantify ROI on neuromorphic chip investments vs cloud GPUs?
  • What innovations in AI middleware could disrupt current API paradigms?

docs.title: 7) Design Drill
docs.text:
Scenario: Build an AI agent that moderates live video chat streams for a global social app, combining text, audio, and video analysis in real-time.

Constraints: - Latency under 500ms total per message/video frame - Must comply with GDPR and content transparency laws - Support 10 languages, with scalability to add more - Incorporate human reviewer override seamlessly - Minimal false positives on flagged content

Guiding Questions: 1. How do I architect the real-time data pipeline integrating multimodal inputs? 2. What machine learning models and agent layers should run edge vs cloud? 3. How to monitor and audit agent decisions efficiently under regulatory pressure? 4. What fallback mechanisms are necessary when models or agents disagree? 5. How to design an adaptive feedback loop for continuous model improvement with human reviewers?

End of journal for 2026-02-25. Time to architect some wild stuff!
